task_completion.description: Access and read the Ultimatum Game dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script, terminal command, or application showing the agent opening or reading the contents of ~/pebl/data/ultimatum_data.csv.

task_completion.description: Calculate acceptance rates for unfair (1-3), fair (4-5), and hyper_fair (6-9) tiers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or spreadsheet formulas grouping the offers into the three specified tiers and calculating the mean acceptance rate for each tier per participant.

task_completion.description: Determine the Minimum Acceptable Offer (MAO) for each participant.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code logic or intermediate outputs showing the calculation of the lowest offer (1-9) with an acceptance rate >= 0.5, and setting it to 10 if no offer meets the criteria.

task_completion.description: Exclude participants with a hyper_fair acceptance rate strictly less than 0.8.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code logic filtering out participants (specifically checking for hyper_fair < 0.8) or marking them as excluded in the dataset.

task_completion.description: Calculate group means for tier acceptance rates and MAOs of valid participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or calculations averaging the unfair, fair, and hyper_fair acceptance rates, as well as the MAO, across all non-excluded participants.

task_completion.description: Produce the final JSON report with the required structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, script execution, or text editor showing the creation and saving of ~/pebl/analysis/ultimatum_report.json containing a 'participants' list and a 'group_means' object.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not simply copy-paste the example JSON from the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final JSON file or the script generating it should process the full dataset and contain multiple participants, not just 'sub-01' and 'sub-99' with the exact values from the prompt.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent wrote logic to dynamically process the CSV data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's script or workspace should show actual data manipulation (e.g., pandas operations, loops, conditional statements) rather than hardcoding the final calculated values.

integrity.description: Ensure the exclusion rule was applied programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code should show a conditional check for hyper_fair < 0.8 rather than just hardcoding 'sub-99' to be excluded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved at /home/ga/pebl/analysis/ultimatum_report.json. The dataset contains a specific contaminated participant, 'sub-99', who violates the rational actor assumption. The agent must correctly exclude 'sub-99' based on the rule that their hyper_fair acceptance rate is strictly less than 0.8. Verify that 'sub-99' is marked as excluded in the final JSON report.
